subject: [PATCH] nfsd: reset write verifier on deferred writeback errors

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer Chuck Lever asked if there's a pending fix for the same issue in the async COPY path and offered to post one if necessary
reviews.raw_body:
Do you have an additional fix pending that addresses the same bug
in the async COPY path? If not, I can post one.


-- 
Chuck Lever

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the error path in nfsd_vfs_write() needs separate handling because it's not called from an nfsd context, and agreed to handle it differently.
sentiment_signals: acknowledged a need for additional work
reviews.raw_body:
No, I don't. I didn't spot that one before.

Handling it separately is probably good. That case isn't being called
from nfsd context, so it'll need different handling.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where filemap_check_wb_err() returns an error due to a writeback failure, and suggested adding a call to commit_reset_write_verifier() in this scenario.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where filemap_check_wb_err() returns -EAGAIN or -ESTALE, and requested adding a check to filter these errors before calling commit_reset_write_verifier().
sentiment_signals: patch needs additional handling for writeback errors
reviews.raw_body:
From: Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

On Fri, 22 May 2026 12:44:19 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:

---

Applied to nfsd-testing, thanks!

[1/1] nfsd: reset write verifier on deferred writeback errors
      commit: f8cf3c1c418ef04947bc16e4a2ef8074452de593

--
Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

2026-05-22.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the NFS server where deferred writeback errors were not properly handled, leading to silent data loss. The issue arises when clients hold UNSTABLE write data under the current verifier and then COMMIT, only to receive the unchanged verifier back and conclude their data is durable. To fix this, the patch adds calls to commit_reset_write_verifier() at two error sites in nfsd_vfs_write() and nfsd_commit(), ensuring that the server's write verifier is properly rotated on deferred writeback errors. This change aligns with existing patterns in the code and ensures compliance with the UNSTABLE+COMMIT durability contract.
